definition: The last opposing candle before a displacement move — the zone where smart money placed their orders.
depends_on: displacement (OB is DEFINED by the displacement that follows it — no displacement, no OB)
feeds_into: optimal_trade_entry (OTE applied within the OB zone)
unicorn_model (broken OB becomes breaker → breaker + FVG = unicorn)
related_to: premium_discount (OB must be in correct zone)
bridge_integration: Order blocks drive scoring, gating, and entry-zone selection. (1) Detection: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:509-512 calls analysis.order_blocks.detect_order_blocks(df, fvgs=fvgs, swings=ltf_swings, lookback=20, require_fvg=True, require_bos=False) on the M15 trigger TF. require_fvg=True is the bridge's enforcement of the 'OB without displacement is just a candle' rule from this card — the upstream detector only emits OBs that have an FVG following them, which is the displacement signature. (2) Scoring: analysis.ict.scorer.score_ict_setup at scorer.py:283-299 awards up to 15 pts (weight['order_block']) — 60% if any active OB in the trade direction sits within 0.8% of price, +40% if best_ob.is_high_quality (full quality validity), +25% if validity_score >= 2 (mid-quality). The result lands on SymbolAnalysis.ob_score and synergy_scorer.py:77 treats ob_score >= 10 as 'OB present'. (3) Synergies and gates: 'OB + FVG overlap' is the headline synergy at +10 (synergy_scorer.py:347, also applied at FVG-detection-time priority sort in ict_pipeline.py:880-886 where FVG+OB stack is priority 3, above plain OTE-FVG and plain FVG); 'OTE in OB/FVG stack' adds +5 (line 359); 'OB at high-volume zone' adds +3 (line 450). The 'Displacement gates OB validity' gate subtracts 4 when ob_score >= 10 but no displacement is confirmed (line 482-486). (4) Hidden Order Blocks (wick-based institutional rejection from analysis.order_blocks.detect_hidden_obs) are detected at ict_pipeline.py:958-965 and added to advanced_factors as 'HiddenOB' when within 0.5% of price; this feeds the 'OB at high-volume zone' synergy via synergy_scorer.py:279. (5) Entry pricing: ict_pipeline.py:880-886 prefers FVG+OB overlap as the highest-priority retracement-FVG zone for the fvg_entry_price (CE) limit-order anchor; claude_decision.py:273-278 surfaces 'OB + FVG stacked — highest probability entry zone' when both ob_score >= 10 and fvg_score >= 9 fire. claude_decision.py:993 prints the OB score in the prompt with 'confirmed' / 'nearby' / 'none' labels so Claude knows whether OB or FVG/structure should anchor the entry. (6) Breaker blocks and mitigation blocks are NOT detected in code — they appear only in concept_injector.py:244-245 ('Failed OB flipped — now acts as support/resistance') as a prompt-injection hint when the upstream advanced_factors list contains the literal string 'breaker'. Currently no factor producer populates that string, so this is informational/aspirational. Mitigation_block has no integration today.
bullish_OB: Last bearish candle before strong bullish displacement. Body (not wicks) = the zone.
bearish_OB: Last bullish candle before strong bearish displacement. Body = the zone.

quality_chain.must_have_displacement: OB without displacement is just a candle. Displacement (layer 3) CREATES the OB.
quality_chain.OB_plus_FVG: If displacement also left a FVG, the OB is highest quality. OB + FVG overlap = best entry zone.
quality_chain.first_touch: OBs strongest on first retest. Each touch mitigates orders. Connects to 'mitigation' concept.
quality_chain.unmitigated: If price already returned and reacted → mitigated (used up). Don't trade it.

breaker_block.definition: A failed OB — price broke through it, invalidating the OB. Now acts as S/R in the OPPOSITE direction.
breaker_block.connection: Breaker blocks feed into unicorn_model when they overlap with a FVG.

mitigation_block.definition: Partially tested OB — some orders filled but not all. Lower reliability than fresh OB.
mitigation_block.connection: Weight lower in scoring. Still valid as confluence with other PD_arrays.
